body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: wg words-as-memories rollout
body.intention: I intend that we verify the wg-to-memories migration did not silently slow queries that touch memories without type filtering.
body.description:
**What changed:** ~57K word-graph words added to clan.memories as WORD_GRAPH type nodes, growing the table from ~128K to ~185K rows.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) tsvector GIN searches on memories.payload and memories.narrative that do NOT filter by memory_type scan 44% more rows — consolidation.py, distillation.py, and any full-text habit search that omits a type clause will regress; (2) any SELECT * on memories without a type filter (e.g. get_by_type with no type arg) silently becomes 44% heavier; (3) spreading activation semantic equivalence: if links_weighted from wg_edges doesn't reproduce wg_edges traversal faithfully, predict_next results drift without an obvious error.
**Signals to watch:** cortex.py query durations in the log (look for traverse_interpretive or get_by_type calls taking > 2× baseline); spreading activation latency benchmark (T-wg-spread-via-cortex sets a <5ms baseline); hit count on idx_memories_parent_id vs full-seq-scan warnings in Postgres slow query log.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-22.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** no tests — observation ticket. Close with one of: CLEAR (no regression observed), OCCURRED (regression found, file follow-on fix ticket), PARTIAL (minor drift, within tolerance).
